title: In Paris café, terror attack survivors find community: 'People want to tell each other: I am alive'
subhead: After long days attending the 2015 Paris attacks trial, survivors and relatives of victims met in a bar to comfort one another. They have continued to gather every month, joined by victims of other attacks, to share experiences, advice and, above all, laughter and good company.

title: 2026 World Cup: Spain clinches second win after Argentina disappoints in final

Hundreds of thousands of Venezuelans need aid after earthquakes: 'We have no income left'
subhead: United Nations agencies estimate 650,000 people, including 230,000 children, have been affected by the devastating earthquakes of June 24, which have now claimed more than 5,000 lives– a figure that is still rising. Aid workers warn that the health emergency is far from over.

title: 'I reread my texts out of fear that they will be shared'
subhead: Le Monde's Guillemette Faure looks at the spread of screenshots of private conversations, a tactic Donald Trump has repeatedly abused.

title: The cinematic and audiovisual influence of Homer's 'Odyssey', from Méliès to Godard
subhead: Homer's ancient Greek epic poem has fueled the imaginations of countless filmmakers, giving rise to a multitude of adaptations, from sword-and-sandal epics to parodies, adult films and even Italian Westerns.

title: Arthur Jafa transforms the African-American experience into visual poetry
subhead: He had to wait until he was 55 to find success. A decade later, Arthur Jafa has become an artist whose work is shown around the world. His videos have a powerful sensory and emotional impact, weaving an African-American narrative shaped by violence and resilience.

title: War is undermining social contract between Putin and Russians
subhead: The spread of Ukrainian attacks across the country is deepening Russians' concerns about the regime's ability to protect their basic needs, as the economic situation deteriorates.

title: In a world in turmoil, 'Hamlet' has never been more relevant
subhead: The very embodiment of choosing chaos over building a better future, the 16th-century Shakespearean character continues to inspire contemporary theater directors.
